HEAL HOUSE · HEALHOUSE.CACONFIDENTIAL

Mission

This Is Not a Spa.
This Is Not a Clinic.

Heal House is an institutional-grade wellness environment — purpose-built residential living where every system, surface, and service is designed around human optimization.

Air. Water. Light. Movement. Recovery. Community. Not as amenities — as architecture.

For Lakeshore. For Toronto.

HEAL HOUSE · HEALHOUSE.CA02 / 14

Vision

A building built around its residents.

Through a non-profit co-ownership model powered by Build Canada Homes (BCH), Heal House unlocks forgivable government loans, 50-year CMHC financing, and a legacy asset that transfers to community ownership — while delivering institutional-grade returns to investors.

Conventional

S1: Mid-Rise

90 units · Standard financing

BCH + NFP

S2: Mid-Rise + BCH

90 units · Forgivable loan · 60/40 split

Conventional

S3: Full Build-Out

220 units + Hotel · Standard financing

★ Recommended

S4: Full + BCH

220 units + Hotel · Forgivable loan · 60/40 · Legacy exit

HEAL HOUSE · HEALHOUSE.CA09 / 14

Social Impact — Non-Profit Partner

Building a Legacy. Not Just a Building.

In BCH scenarios, 40% of net cash flow accrues to the Non-Profit Partner. At Year 20, the entire asset transfers to community ownership.

20-Year NFP Income (S4)

—

Cumulative 40% share of net cash flow over 20 years

Legacy Asset Value at Year 20

—

Building + land transfers in full to the Non-Profit Partner

What the 40% Funds

🏠 Affordable housing preservation — Maintaining below-market units for essential workers and seniors

🤝 Community programming — Wellness workshops, mental health services, youth mentorship

🏥 Tenant services — On-site health coordination, preventative care access, community kitchen

🌱 Environmental stewardship — Green building maintenance, urban agriculture, sustainability initiatives

Income Split — 60% Investor / 40% Non-Profit

60% INVESTOR
40% NFP
HEAL HOUSE · HEALHOUSE.CA10 / 14

Market Opportunity

The market is ready. The need is undeniable.

$5.6T

Global Longevity Economy

The fastest-growing consumer segment on Earth. By 2030, adults 60+ will drive $5.6 trillion in annual spending.

89%

Burnout Crisis

89% of Canadian workers report burnout. Mental health claims are up 300% since 2019. Prevention, not treatment, is the future.

0

Direct Competitors

No purpose-built wellness-integrated residential asset exists in Canada. Not condo. Not co-living. A new category.

Healthcare systems are collapsing under treatment costs. Heal House is a prevention infrastructure — residential living that reduces hospital visits, improves outcomes, and generates returns for investors and communities alike.
